event_blocks.summary: The player, controlling Phoenix and wielding a Vandal rifle, navigates a dimly lit A Main corridor amidst intense Valorant gameplay, with teammates calling out enemy positions and warning, "Watch your eyes." Phoenix swiftly eliminates multiple opponents, confirmed by shouts of "Dead!" The player activates their 'Run It Back' ability, marked by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, as the environment transitions to an open outdoor area featuring a gelato shop. Further gunshots and a "Nice!" confirm successful eliminations, followed by a callout, "Enemy spike down A," and a command to "Rush!" The player secures an 'ACE' and later performs a 'CLUTCH' in a subsequent round, surviving with low health after audio cues like "One enemy remaining." A commentary-style intro begins, noting Phoenix's kills. The Vandal rifle later sports a gold and black skin, and Japanese HUD text, such as 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier defeated), appears alongside Japanese voice lines from Phoenix like "I will mark it" and "一人じゃない" (You are not alone), followed by "敵を倒した" (Enemy defeated). The player's health fluctuates dramatically during combat, with encouragement like "Survive" and Phoenix saying, "気をつけて" (Be careful). The player secures a win, with 'WON' and 'CLUTCH' banners appearing, accompanied by audio cues like "キャリアーダウン" (Carrier down) and Phoenix exclaiming, "さあ、行くぞ" (Let's go!), culminating in a final callout, "残り1人だ" (One left), highlighting skillful performance under pressure, with the mini-map consistently displaying strategic positioning.

Mini-map.description: A circular overhead map in the top-left corner showing player positions, site locations (A Main, B Site), and enemy movements. Consistently displayed throughout the gameplay, aiding in tactical navigation.

Gelato Shop.description: A colorful storefront with a sign reading 'GELATO' in the outdoor area, serving as a visual landmark during combat. Features a striped awning and ice cream display, adding to the Mediterranean aesthetic of the map.

Vandal Rifle.description: A primary weapon with a dynamic skin that changes from a blue and pink dragon design to a gold and black design. Its distinctive rapid gunfire sounds are prominent throughout the combat sequences, as it's used for eliminating enemies with high precision in both indoor and outdoor environments.

Phoenix.description: The player character, identifiable by fiery orange hands during ability use and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem on the back. Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a blue and pink dragon skin, and later a gold and black skin. Uses 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects. Phoenix is heard speaking Japanese lines such as '一人じゃない' (You are not alone), '敵を倒した' (Enemy defeated), '気をつけて' (Be careful), and 'さあ、行くぞ' (Let's go!), alongside grunts and combat sounds.

Run It Back Ability.description: Phoenix’s signature ability, visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, and accompanied by a distinct sound effect upon activation. It is used multiple times to recover from near-death situations, with a cooldown bar visible on the HUD.

Japanese HUD Text.description: On-screen text in Japanese, such as 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier defeated) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. This visual shift is complemented by spoken Japanese phrases in the audio, including Phoenix's own dialogue and callouts like 'キャリアーダウン' (Carrier down), which aligns with the visual notification of a spike carrier being defeated.

raw_segments: The minute opens with intense Valorant gameplay, as the player, controlling Phoenix and wielding a Vandal rifle with a striking blue and pink dragon skin, navigates a dimly lit A Main corridor. Gunshots ring out as teammates call out enemy positions, "Two mid, two A main," and warn, "Watch your eyes." Phoenix swiftly eliminates multiple opponents, accompanied by shouts of "Dead!" and "Careful now." The player activates their signature 'Run It Back' ability, visually marked by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, as a teammate urges, "Come on, let's go!" The environment transitions to an open outdoor area with Mediterranean architecture, including a gelato shop. More gunshots and a "Nice!" confirm successful eliminations, followed by a callout, "Enemy spike down A," and a command to "Rush!" The player secures an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies, then performs a 'CLUTCH' in a subsequent round, surviving with low health. Audio cues like "One enemy remaining" and "One drop" precede the clutch. A voice exclaims, "Hey!" and then, "That was actually insane," transitioning into a commentary-style intro: "Today's episode... Hello guys and welcome back to another video of Valorant. Today we're watching Rauzu on kill some enemies." He notes, "He's got three kills and four. Wow," before reminding viewers to "Don't forget to like the video and subscribe." As the gameplay continues, the Vandal rifle now sports a gold and black skin, and Japanese HUD text, such as 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier defeated), begins to appear, replacing English text. Gunshots are prominent, mixed with Japanese voice lines from Phoenix: "I will mark it," "Mark," and "一人じゃない" (You are not alone), followed by "敵を倒した" (Enemy defeated). The player's health fluctuates dramatically during combat. A voice urges, "Survive," and Phoenix says, "気をつけて" (Be careful). Another voice encourages, "You have gun now, you have gun, go, easy, yes." The player secures a win, with 'WON' and 'CLUTCH' banners appearing on screen. The audio includes "This is like survival," "キャリアーダウン" (Carrier down), Phoenix exclaiming, "さあ、行くぞ" (Let's go!), and a final callout, "残り1人だ" (One left), emphasizing their skillful performance under pressure. The mini-map consistently shows strategic positioning throughout.

event_blocks.summary: The video transitions from a black screen with the text 'I want to give a big shoutout' to vibrant first-person shooter gameplay, likely Valorant, set in an outdoor Mediterranean-style environment near a Gelato shop, accompanied by upbeat electronic music. A male voice introduces the sponsor, Blitz.gg, explaining its purpose for improving in Valorant and recommending it. The player, Phoenix, wielding a gold and black Vandal rifle, engages in combat, with the Mini-map tracking movements. The voiceover highlights Blitz.gg's tracking of player strengths and weaknesses across maps, agents, and weapons. Phoenix utilizes his 'Run It Back' ability, characterized by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, to recover from a critical situation. The scene shifts to a post-match analysis screen, detailing aim accuracy, utility usage, and round-by-round stats, emphasizing actual learning over simply playing. Promotional text overlays for Blitz.gg appear, showcasing its analytics. The video then moves to a character selection screen, where the voiceover explains the visibility of team and enemy stats, including K/D, headshot percentage, and win rate, during agent select and on the loading screen. The HUD displays Japanese text, such as 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier defeated). The final scenes show Phoenix in a purple-lit indoor area, eliminating opponents with the Vandal, while the voiceover mentions Blitz.gg's live mid-game overlays and support for various games like League, Marvel Rivals, CS2, Fortnite, and Palworld, encouraging viewers to download the Blitz desktop app for PC via a provided link.

Mini-map.description: A circular overhead map in the top-left corner showing player positions, site locations (A Main, B Site), and enemy movements. Consistently displayed throughout the gameplay, aiding in tactical navigation.

Gelato Shop.description: A colorful storefront with a sign reading 'GELATO' in the outdoor area, serving as a visual landmark during combat. Features a striped awning and ice cream display, adding to the Mediterranean aesthetic of the map.

Vandal Rifle.description: A primary weapon with a dynamic skin that changes to a gold and black design. Used for eliminating enemies with high precision, visible during both outdoor and indoor combat sequences.

Phoenix.description: The player character, identifiable by fiery orange hands during ability use and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem on the back. Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a gold and black skin. Uses 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects.

Blitz.gg Interface.description: A performance-tracking overlay and post-match analysis screen displaying detailed statistics such as Combat Score, Headshot %, Damage Per Round, and team coordination metrics. Features a dark theme with green and purple accents, and includes promotional text for Blitz.gg's analytics across multiple games.

Run It Back Ability.description: Phoenix’s signature ability, visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect. Activated multiple times to recover from near-death situations, with a cooldown bar visible on the HUD.

Character Selection Screen.description: A menu interface showing Phoenix as a Duelist with a description highlighting his aggressive playstyle, displaying agent icons, win rates, and match history. The male voiceover refers to this as 'agent select,' explaining that it shows 'team stats, K/D, headshot percentage, win rate' and even enemy team stats on the loading screen, aiding tactical decisions before a match.

Promotional Text Overlays.description: On-screen text in bold white font with black outlines, promoting Blitz.gg with phrases like 'Blitz tracks literally everything', 'where you’re not', and 'for pc using my link'. Includes animated emojis like a thinking face and a frog, adding a playful tone to the sponsorship message.

Japanese HUD Text.description: On-screen text in Japanese, such as 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier defeated) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. Appears in later frames, replacing English text.

raw_segments: The video opens with a black screen displaying the text 'I want to give a big shoutout' before transitioning into vibrant, colorful first-person shooter gameplay, likely Valorant, set in an outdoor Mediterranean-style environment near a Gelato shop. An upbeat electronic background music track begins as a male voice introduces the sponsor, Blitz.gg, explaining its purpose: "How do I actually improve in Valorant? And honestly, this is what I would recommend." The player, Phoenix, wielding a dynamic gold and black Vandal rifle, engages in combat while the Mini-map tracks movements. The voiceover details how "Blitz tracks literally everything: your best maps, agents, weapons, and more," showing "exactly where you're strong and, more importantly, where you're not." Phoenix uses his 'Run It Back' ability, visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, to recover from a near-death situation. The scene shifts to a post-match analysis screen, which the voiceover describes as a "full breakdown: aim accuracy, utility usage, round-by-round stats," emphasizing that "you're not just vibing through games, you're actually learning." Promotional text overlays for Blitz.gg appear, highlighting its analytics. The video then cuts to a character selection screen, where the voiceover explains that "during agent select, you'll see your team stats, K/D, headshot percentage, win rate," and even enemy team stats on the loading screen. The HUD displays Japanese text like 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier defeated). The final frames return to Phoenix navigating a purple-lit indoor area, eliminating enemies with the Vandal, while the voiceover concludes by mentioning Blitz.gg's "live overlays mid-game" and support for "League, Marvel Rivals, CS2, Fortnite, Palworld, and many more," urging viewers to "download the Blitz desktop app for PC using my link."

event_blocks.summary: Phoenix, clad in a white jacket with an orange flame emblem and sporting fiery orange hands, navigates diverse Valorant environments, from sunlit Mediterranean alleys near a Gelato Shop to sterile, high-tech indoor sites and snowy outdoor areas with industrial structures. Wielding a Vandal rifle, initially with a dynamic blue and pink skin and later a gold and black one, he engages enemies with rapid gunfire, punctuated by English and Japanese callouts like "scatter!", "残り1名" (one remaining), and "気をつけな" (be careful). Phoenix frequently utilizes his 'Run It Back' ability, visually indicated by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect accompanied by a distinct whooshing sound, to recover from near-death situations and re-engage, often followed by a sigh. The Mini-map consistently tracks enemy positions and site locations like 'A Site', 'B Site', 'B Link', 'C Long', and 'C Site'. Japanese HUD text, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), and Japanese voice lines like "スパイクを設置" (Spike planted) and "敵残り1名" (Enemy remaining: 1) are interspersed with English comms like "Let me ace, let me ace guys, please" and "They're super dead." He secures an 'ACE' after activating 'Run It Back' near the Gelato Shop, exclaiming "次行くよ" (Let's go next) in Japanese. Later, at A Site, he plants the Spike, reloads with "リロード中" (Reloading), and eliminates opponents with precise Vandal fire, declaring "仕留めたぜ" (I got him) and "ねぇ、気をつけな" (Hey, be careful). The gameplay showcases dynamic lighting, particle effects, and Phoenix's aggressive playstyle, culminating in clutch plays and teammate cheers like "Ace, ace!"

Mini-map.description: A circular overhead map in the top-left corner showing player positions, site locations (C Long, C Site, A Site), and enemy movements. Consistently displayed throughout the gameplay, aiding in tactical navigation and site control tracking.

Gelato Shop.description: A colorful storefront with a sign reading 'GELATO' in the outdoor area, serving as a visual landmark during combat. Features a striped awning and ice cream display, adding to the Mediterranean aesthetic of the map.

Vandal Rifle.description: A primary weapon with a dynamic skin that changes from a blue and pink design to a gold and black design. Its distinct gunfire sounds are heard throughout, used for eliminating enemies with high precision during both outdoor and indoor combat sequences, including a close-range engagement after planting the Spike.

Phoenix.description: The player character, identifiable by fiery orange hands during ability use and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem on the back. Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a dynamic skin that changes from blue and pink to gold and black. Uses 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects, and secures an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies. He speaks both English ("scatter!", "Flash!", "No enemies!", "The hell?", "Oh, he's gone.") and Japanese ("Né, ganbatte", "次行くよ", "ほら、受け取って", "リロード中", "仕留めたぜ", "ねぇ、気をつけな", "ここ", "ない"), and grunts during combat.

Run It Back Ability.description: Phoenix’s signature ability, visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect. Activated multiple times to recover from near-death situations, with a cooldown bar visible on the HUD, allowing him to secure an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies, a moment celebrated by excited shouts from teammates.

Promotional Text Overlays.description: On-screen text in bold white font with black outlines, promoting Blitz.gg with phrases like 'and super easy to use' and accompanied by a meme-style frog character, is introduced by a male English voiceover stating, "It's completely free and super easy to use," setting a playful, sponsored tone.

Japanese HUD Text.description: On-screen text in Japanese, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. Appears in later frames, replacing English text and providing real-time game status updates, sometimes coinciding with Phoenix's Japanese dialogue.

raw_segments: The video opens with a blurred, colorful scene featuring a meme-style frog character and the promotional text 'and super easy to use', accompanied by a male English voiceover stating, "It's completely free and super easy to use." This quickly transitions into fast-paced first-person Valorant gameplay, where the player, Phoenix, navigates futuristic indoor environments with teal and white walls, wielding a Vandal rifle with a gold and black skin. Gunfire and ability sounds, including Phoenix's distinct fiery whooshing for his 'Run It Back' ability, punctuate the action as he engages enemies. The Mini-map in the top-left corner consistently displays team positions and site locations like 'A Site' and 'B Link'. Phoenix uses 'Run It Back' multiple times, visually indicated by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, to recover from near-death situations, with a cooldown bar visible. Japanese HUD text, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), is complemented by in-game Japanese voice lines like "グレネード" (Grenade), "レイナを倒した" (Killed Reyna), "スパイクを設置" (Spike planted), "敵残り1名" (Enemy remaining: 1), and "気をつけな" (Be careful). Player comms in English, including "Let me ace, let me ace guys, please," are also heard. The environment dynamically shifts from indoor corridors to an outdoor snowy area with blue 'KNG' shipping containers, and then, after a brief respawn, to a sunlit Mediterranean-style urban area featuring a Gelato shop, where Phoenix continues to eliminate opponents with precise Vandal fire. The sequence concludes with Phoenix activating 'Run It Back' again after being hit, showcasing its visual effects amidst intense combat, dynamic lighting, and particle effects.

The minute opens with Phoenix, identifiable by his white jacket with an orange flame emblem, engaging in intense combat at B Site on a vibrant, Mediterranean-style map. Wielding his gold and black Vandal rifle, he eliminates an enemy amidst the sounds of precise gunfire and a teammate's confident callout, "They're super dead." As he faces a critical moment, Phoenix activates his 'Run It Back' ability, visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, accompanied by a distinct whooshing sound, allowing him to recover. The Mini-map in the top-left corner consistently tracks enemy positions and site locations. The environment shifts from bright blue skies outdoors to warm, orange-hued indoor corridors. As the gameplay progresses, the HUD text changes to Japanese, with a game announcer declaring "最後のひとりだ" (Last one) after a kill. Phoenix moves to a colorful Gelato Shop area, continuing to engage enemies with his Vandal, its gunfire echoing through the space. Teammates provide callouts like "What flashes, please. Not enough," and Phoenix himself exclaims "悪いな" (My bad) and "Triple da!" (Triple kill!) during the skirmishes. He then transitions to A Site, where he again uses his 'Run It Back' ability with its signature sound to survive, showcasing his aggressive playstyle. The Japanese game announcer confirms "スパイクを設置" (Spike planted) and Phoenix's own voice is heard saying "仕留めたぜ" (I got him). The segment concludes with Phoenix securing more kills, his Vandal's gunfire punctuating the action, and the Japanese game announcer declaring "敵を" (Enemy [eliminated]), demonstrating tactical precision and effective ability usage against the backdrop of colorful buildings and dynamic lighting.

The minute opens with intense tactical shooter gameplay, following Phoenix, a Duelist with fiery orange hands and a white jacket bearing an orange flame emblem, as he navigates diverse environments. The distinct rapid gunfire of his gold and black Vandal rifle punctuates the action, accompanied by a mix of English and Japanese callouts from teammates and Phoenix himself, such as "残り1名" (one remaining) and "気をつけな" (be careful). Phoenix pushes forward, eliminating enemies with precision, his movements guided by the Mini-map in the top-left corner. Japanese HUD text like 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) appears, reinforcing the regional server setting. He frequently activates his signature "Run It Back" ability, visually marked by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects, often followed by a deep, audible sigh, allowing him to recover from near-death situations and re-engage. Dialogue like "I can push" and "ultimate is ready" ("アルティメットいけるぜ") from Phoenix highlights his aggressive playstyle. The combat shifts from indoor corridors with blue and beige walls to snowy outdoor areas featuring industrial structures and a vibrant Gelato Shop. As Phoenix plants the spike at B Site, a voice announces "スパイクを設置" (spike planted). Moments later, he exclaims "I'm getting flanked, I'm cooked!" amidst more Vandal gunfire and frantic callouts like "above you, above you." The sequence culminates with an announcer declaring "Last player standing" and Phoenix's teammates cheering "Ace, ace!" as he survives a critical encounter, showcasing his reliance on his time-reversal ability and relentless aggression.

The minute begins with Phoenix, identifiable by his white jacket with an orange flame emblem and fiery orange hands, navigating sunlit, Mediterranean-style outdoor alleys near the Gelato Shop. Wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic blue and pink skin, he engages enemies with rapid gunfire, exclaiming "scatter!" and "No enemies!" in English, while also uttering "Né, ganbatte" in Japanese. The Mini-map in the top-left corner tracks his position near C Long and C Site. After a "Flash!" callout, an announcer confirms "One enemy remaining." Phoenix then activates his 'Run It Back' ability, visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, recovering from a near-death situation. This moment is met with excited shouts from teammates, "OH! Get him! Give me the ACE!", as Phoenix secures an 'ACE' by eliminating all remaining enemies, then states "次行くよ" in Japanese. The scene transitions to a sterile, high-tech indoor environment, likely A Site, where Phoenix switches to a Vandal with a gold and black skin. He plants the Spike, accompanied by the Japanese HUD text 'スパイク設置完了' and his own Japanese dialogue "ほら、受け取って". Engaging in close-quarters combat, he reloads, announcing "リロード中", and eliminates enemies with precise gunfire, declaring "仕留めたぜ" and "ねぇ、気をつけな". Further Japanese HUD text, 'プライマリウェポン使用不能', appears as he continues to navigate tight corridors, securing the site with an aggressive playstyle, punctuated by his grunts and calls of "ここ" and "ない".

event_blocks.summary: Phoenix, distinguished by his white jacket with an orange flame emblem and wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic gold and black skin, advanced through a futuristic indoor corridor towards A Site, as indicated by the Mini-map. Amidst gunfire and a Japanese announcement of "Enemy remaining: 1 person," followed by "Gadra," he entered an outdoor Mediterranean-style area featuring a Gelato Shop. Phoenix planted the Spike, confirmed by Japanese HUD text 'スパイク設置完了' and his own "Spike planted," accompanied by the arming sound. He then engaged in combat, securing eliminations with rapid gunfire and exclaiming "Aha!" He activated his 'Run It Back' ability, marked by glowing orange fists, a time-reversal effect, and the declaration "Here for hellfire!" to recover. The Vandal rifle's skin shifted to blue and pink as he achieved an 'ACE' with precise headshots. Throughout the firefight, "No charge/ammo" and "Useless" were heard in Japanese, alongside "Spike down A" and "Last player standing." After being downed, the Japanese HUD text displayed 'Primary weapon unusable.' In a final close-range engagement, Phoenix, now using a melee weapon, secured the 'ACE' with a finishing blow, accompanied by a melee hit sound and his "Aha!" The environment, a blend of modern and traditional design with dynamic lighting and purple and orange glows, was filled with continuous combat sounds and player communication.

Mini-map.description: A circular overhead map in the top-left corner showing player positions, site locations (C Long, C Site, A Site), and enemy movements. Consistently displayed throughout the gameplay, aiding in tactical navigation and site control tracking.

Gelato Shop.description: A colorful storefront with a sign reading 'GELATO' in the outdoor area, serving as a visual landmark during combat. Features a striped awning and ice cream display, adding to the Mediterranean aesthetic of the map.

Vandal Rifle.description: A primary weapon with a dynamic skin that changes from a blue and pink design to a gold and black design. It is used for eliminating enemies with high precision, visible during both outdoor and indoor combat sequences, and produces distinct, rapid gunfire sounds throughout the segment.

Phoenix.description: The player character, identifiable by fiery orange hands during ability use and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem on the back. Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a dynamic skin that changes from blue and pink to gold and black. He uses his 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects, and secures an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies. Phoenix is highly vocal, speaking Japanese with lines like "スパイクを設置" (Spike planted), "チャージがねぇ" (No charge/ammo), and "無駄" (Useless), and English with a triumphant "Aha!" after eliminations and "Here for hellfire!" when activating his ultimate. He also grunts during combat.

Run It Back Ability.description: Phoenix’s signature ability, visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect. Activated multiple times to recover from near-death situations, with a cooldown bar visible on the HUD. It is accompanied by a unique, distinct sound effect and Phoenix's specific voice line, "Here for hellfire!", allowing him to secure an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies.

Japanese HUD Text.description: On-screen text in Japanese, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. This text appears in later frames, replacing English text and providing real-time game status updates, often correlating with Phoenix's own Japanese voice lines.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Phoenix, identifiable by his white jacket with an orange flame emblem, swiftly navigating a futuristic indoor corridor while wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic gold and black skin. The Mini-map in the top-left corner tracks his progression towards A Site amidst the sounds of gunfire and a Japanese voice announcing "敵残り1名" (Enemy remaining: 1 person), followed by an English callout "Gadra." As he approaches, the environment transitions to an outdoor Mediterranean-style area featuring a Gelato Shop, where Phoenix plants the Spike, confirmed by the Japanese HUD text 'スパイク設置完了' and his own Japanese voice line "スパイクを設置" (Spike planted), accompanied by the distinct sound of the Spike being armed. He immediately engages in combat, with rapid gunfire and his triumphant "Aha!" after eliminations. Phoenix utilizes his 'Run It Back' ability, visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, and audibly by a unique sound effect and his declaration "Here for hellfire!", to recover from a near-death situation. The Vandal rifle's skin dynamically shifts to a vibrant blue and pink design as he eliminates multiple enemies with precise headshots, securing an 'ACE'. Throughout the intense firefight, various English and Japanese voice lines are heard, including Phoenix stating "チャージがねぇ" (No charge/ammo) and "無駄" (Useless), and an announcer confirming "Spike down A" and "Last player standing." After being downed, the Japanese HUD text updates to 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable). The scene culminates in a dramatic close-range engagement where Phoenix, now with a melee weapon, finishes off the last enemy, highlighted by the 'ACE' notification, a final melee hit sound, and his concluding "Aha!". The environment maintains its blend of modern and traditional design, with dynamic lighting and purple and orange glows from abilities and gunfire, all underscored by the continuous sounds of combat and player communication.

event_blocks.summary: Phoenix, distinguished by his white jacket and orange flame emblem, initiated combat on a vibrant, Mediterranean-inspired map, wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic blue and pink skin. Amidst intense gunfire and distinct ability sounds, he declared, "You think you're safe," before activating his 'Run It Back' ability, visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, accompanied by a distinct sound, to recover from a near-death situation. He secured an 'ACE' with precise Vandal shots, prompting excited player reactions and Japanese game announcer callouts like "やりやがった" (You did it!) and "アルティメットいけるぜ" (Ultimate ready!). As Phoenix navigated from the Gelato Shop area to industrial zones, the Mini-map tracked his movements and enemy positions, with players making callouts like "I got enemies B" and "3B." He announced, "My ultimate ready!" and later, "I have ult!," before using a flash ability with a "Flash!" callout. The environment transitioned to a lush, green A Site, where Japanese HUD text, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted), appeared. Phoenix's combat culminated in a close-range melee finish, prompting the player to exclaim, "Oh no way!" and comment, "He's like, 'fuck this shit!'". The game announcer declared, "One enemy remaining!" and "Last player standing!" as Phoenix eliminated the final foe, leading to a decisive victory with the screen flashing 'WON', accompanied by the player's satisfied "Oh my god, nice!" and the announcer's "Spike down B!". The dynamic lighting shifted from bright daylight to shadowed spaces, enhancing the combat atmosphere throughout. Later, in a narrow C Site corridor, Phoenix, wielding a blue and pink Vandal, engaged enemies with precise gunfire. After a player exclaimed, "Watch your eyes!" and "Oh my god!," Phoenix activated his 'Run It Back' ability, declaring in Japanese, "どこにいるか分かってるぜ" (I know where you are!), before securing another 'ACE' amidst more gunshots and a call of "Last!". The environment shifted to an outdoor Mediterranean-style area with the Gelato Shop. Japanese dialogue continued with a player warning, "おい、気をつけな!" (Hey, watch out!), followed by a game announcer stating, "残り1名、最後の1人だ。" (One remaining, the last one.) and "Bでキャリアダウン" (Carrier down at B.). Phoenix, now with a gold and black Vandal, continued his aggressive playstyle, exclaiming, "Gotcha, son!" and "My turn!" during intense exchanges. As a player noted, "Of course she's behind us," and urged, "Go play, go play," Phoenix planted the Spike, stating, "I'm planting," confirmed by the announcer's "Spike planted." The lighting transitioned from warm indoor tones to bright outdoor sunlight, enhancing the visual contrast as Phoenix dominated the match.

Mini-map.description: A circular overhead map in the top-left corner showing player positions, site locations (C Long, C Site, A Site), and enemy movements. Consistently displayed throughout the gameplay, aiding in tactical navigation and site control tracking.

Gelato Shop.description: A colorful storefront with a sign reading 'GELATO' in the outdoor area, serving as a visual landmark during combat. Features a striped awning and ice cream display, adding to the Mediterranean aesthetic of the map.

Vandal Rifle.description: A primary weapon with a dynamic skin that changes from a blue and pink design to a gold and black design. Its precise gunfire is a consistent auditory element throughout the combat sequences, used for eliminating enemies with high precision, visible during both outdoor and indoor combat sequences, including a close-range engagement after planting the Spike.

Phoenix.description: The player character, identifiable by fiery orange hands during ability use and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem on the back. Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a dynamic skin that changes from blue and pink to gold and black. He uses his 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects, accompanied by a distinct activation sound. His aggressive playstyle is evident through his English dialogue ("Gotcha, son!", "My turn!") and Japanese declarations ("どこにいるか分かってるぜ" - I know where you are!). He secures an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies.

Run It Back Ability.description: Phoenix’s signature ability, visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect. Activated multiple times to recover from near-death situations, it is accompanied by a distinct, recognizable activation sound, with a cooldown bar visible on the HUD, allowing him to secure an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies.

Japanese HUD Text.description: On-screen text in Japanese, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), indicating a language change or regional server setting. This is reinforced by the presence of Japanese dialogue from Phoenix and game announcers, providing real-time game status updates.

Blitz.gg Interface.description: A performance-tracking overlay and post-match analysis screen displaying detailed statistics such as Combat Score, Headshot %, Damage Per Round, and team coordination metrics. Features a dark theme with green and purple accents, and includes promotional text for Blitz.gg's analytics across multiple games.

Character Selection Screen.description: A menu interface showing Phoenix as a Duelist with a description highlighting his aggressive playstyle. Displays agent icons, win rates, and match history, with a blue and white color scheme and a 'BLITZ.GG' watermark.

Promotional Text Overlays.description: On-screen text in bold white font with black outlines, promoting Blitz.gg with phrases like 'and super easy to use', accompanied by a meme-style frog character with a thinking expression, adding a playful tone to the sponsorship message.

A Site Environment.description: A lush, green outdoor area with overgrown vegetation, stone pathways, and ancient-style architecture, contrasting with the earlier Mediterranean-style B Site. Features vibrant lighting and natural elements like moss-covered walls and water features, enhancing the visual atmosphere during combat.

raw_segments: The intense Valorant match opens with Phoenix, identifiable by his white jacket and orange flame emblem, engaging in a chaotic firefight at A Site, wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic blue and pink skin. Gunshots ring out as he eliminates multiple enemies, culminating in an 'ACE' notification and the announcer exclaiming "ACE!". Japanese HUD text, including 'スパイクキャリアーが倒された' (Spike carrier eliminated), flashes on screen. Phoenix then navigates to B Link, where he activates his 'Run It Back' ability, his hands glowing fiery orange as he declares, "I'm a believer! I'm a believer!" The time-reversal effect allows him to recover from near-death and secure another kill amidst the sounds of combat and an announcer stating, "One enemy remaining." The scene shifts to a traditional Japanese-style courtyard, where Phoenix plants the Spike, confirmed by the Japanese HUD text 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and the announcer's "Spike planted." More gunshots and ability sounds punctuate the fight, with Phoenix exclaiming, "My ultimate ready! My turn!" as he uses 'Run It Back' again to survive a close-range engagement near the colorful Gelato Shop. The Mini-map in the top-left corner continuously tracks the evolving battle. The sequence climaxes in a final, chaotic firefight at A Main, where Phoenix, after being downed, reactivates 'Run It Back' with a powerful sound effect and his voice shouting, "Oh my god!" to reverse time, heal, and eliminate the remaining enemies, securing a 'WON' screen as the enemy team is eliminated.

The segment opens with Phoenix, identifiable by his white jacket and orange flame emblem, activating his 'Run It Back' ability, marked by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect, as a male player voice comments, "Uh, yeah, seven on four, not hey." Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a dynamic blue and pink to gold and black skin, Phoenix engages in intense combat near the colorful Gelato Shop. Gunfire erupts as a robotic female voice announces, "No charges left," and Phoenix himself shouts, "Watch your eyes!" He eliminates multiple enemies, securing an 'ACE,' prompting the player to exclaim, "Oh, holy moly!" and "How are you alive? What's going on?" The Mini-map guides his movements as he navigates the Mediterranean-themed outdoor areas. Japanese HUD text appears, displaying 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted), accompanied by Japanese agent voice lines like "グレネード!" (Grenade!) and "爆弾いくぞ!" (Going with the bomb!), followed by the robotic announcer stating, "Spike down A" and "Spike planted." Phoenix continues to use 'Run It Back' multiple times, its cooldown visible, as the player instructs, "Focus." The scene briefly transitions to the Blitz.gg Interface, showcasing detailed stats with a dark theme, and then to the Character Selection Screen highlighting Phoenix as a Duelist, with promotional text overlays like 'and super easy to use' featuring a meme-style frog. The segment concludes with Phoenix successfully defusing the Spike at B Site in an industrial setting, confirmed by the 'ALLY DEFUSING' notification and the robotic announcer declaring, "Enemy down" amidst continued gunfire.

The segment opens with Phoenix, in his white jacket and fiery orange hands, rapidly navigating the Mediterranean-style B Site, wielding a Vandal rifle with a dynamic blue and pink to gold and black skin. Gunshots and distinct ability sounds punctuate his movements as the Mini-map tracks enemy positions. After eliminating an enemy in B Main, triggering a 'SPIKE CARRIER KILLED' notification, he pushes into the open Mid Market, securing another kill amidst more gunfire. The HUD displays Japanese text like 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable) as the announcer declares "One enemy remaining." Phoenix then activates his 'Run It Back' ability, visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, allowing him to recover and secure an "ACE!" The player exclaims "Nice!" and other excited phrases in a foreign language, followed by "You're not good, bro! I'm so good!" as he continues his dominant play. The announcer states "Spike down A" as the scene transitions to the lush, green A Main. Phoenix plants the Spike at A Rubble, with the player saying "Got the spike," "Go plant," and "Plant the spike," which is confirmed by the announcer's "Spike planted." He continues to eliminate enemies with precise Vandal shots, accompanied by intense gunshots and player reactions like a sigh and "Oh no," culminating in a final kill that brings up the 'WON' screen. Throughout, the Blitz.gg interface overlays performance stats, and playful promotional text with a meme-style frog character appears, adding a sponsored touch.

The segment opens with Phoenix, a Duelist identifiable by his white jacket with an orange flame emblem, engaging in intense combat on a vibrant, Mediterranean-inspired map. Gunshots from his Vandal rifle, which sports a dynamic blue and pink skin, punctuate the action as he reacts with an audible "Oh no" to an unseen threat. He confidently declares, "You think you're safe," before activating his 'Run It Back' ability, visually marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, accompanied by a distinct sound, to recover from a near-death situation. The Vandal's precise gunshots are heard as Phoenix eliminates enemies, securing an 'ACE' amidst Japanese game announcer callouts like "やりやがった" (You did it!) and "アルティメットいけるぜ" (Ultimate ready!), and the player's excited "Oh my god!". As Phoenix navigates from the colorful Gelato Shop area to industrial zones, the Mini-map in the top-left corner tracks his tactical movements and enemy positions, with the player making callouts like "I got enemies B" and "3B". He announces, "My ultimate ready!" and later, "I have ult!", before using a flash ability with a "Flash!" callout. The environment transitions to a lush, green A Site. Japanese HUD text, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted), appears, reinforcing the regional server setting. Phoenix's combat culminates in a close-range melee finish, prompting the player to exclaim, "Oh no way!" and comment, "He's like, 'fuck this shit!'". The game announcer declares, "One enemy remaining!" and "Last player standing!" as Phoenix eliminates the final foe, leading to a decisive victory with the screen flashing 'WON', accompanied by the player's satisfied "Oh my god, nice!" and the announcer's "Spike down B!". The dynamic lighting shifts from bright daylight to shadowed spaces, enhancing the combat atmosphere throughout.

The segment opens with Phoenix, identifiable by his white jacket and orange flame emblem, in a narrow C Site corridor, wielding a blue and pink Vandal. Gunshots ring out as a player asks, "Where's the last guy?" and urges, "Come on, let's go!" Phoenix engages enemies with precise gunfire, punctuated by ability sounds. After a player exclaims, "Watch your eyes!" and "Oh my god!", Phoenix activates his 'Run It Back' ability, marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, accompanied by a distinct activation sound. He then declares in Japanese, "どこにいるか分かってるぜ" (I know where you are!), before securing an 'ACE' amidst more gunshots and a call of "Last!". The environment shifts to an outdoor Mediterranean-style area with the Gelato Shop. Japanese dialogue continues with a player warning, "おい、気をつけな!" (Hey, watch out!), followed by a game announcer stating, "残り1名、最後の1人だ。" (One remaining, the last one.) and "Bでキャリアダウン" (Carrier down at B.), to which a player responds, "Nice." Phoenix, now with a gold and black Vandal, continues his aggressive playstyle, engaging in close-range combat with rapid gunfire. He exclaims, "Gotcha, son!" and "My turn!" during intense exchanges, with another player adding, "I know you don't!" and "Nice!". As a player notes, "Of course she's behind us," and urges, "Go play, go play," Phoenix plants the Spike, stating, "I'm planting." The Japanese HUD text for 'Spike planted' appears, confirmed by an English game announcer's "Spike planted." The lighting transitions from warm indoor tones to bright outdoor sunlight, enhancing the visual contrast as Phoenix dominates the match.

event_blocks.summary: Phoenix, clad in a white jacket with an orange flame emblem and wielding a blue and pink Vandal rifle, navigated the B Site of a Mediterranean-style map near a Gelato shop, responding to callouts like "He's behind us" amidst gunfire, securing a kill, and witnessing a 'SPIKE PLANTED' notification. He then activated his 'Run It Back' ability, characterized by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, recovering from a precarious situation and ultimately achieving an 'ACE' amidst player reactions of "Nice" and "Oof." The scene shifted to a Japanese server, evidenced by HUD text such as 'スパイク設置完了' and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能', where Phoenix, now equipped with a gold and black Vandal, advanced through a modern industrial corridor. His voice shifted to Japanese, uttering "気をつけな" (Be careful), "残り1名" (One enemy remaining), and "パーフェクト" (Perfect) during intense close-quarters combat, punctuated by continuous gunfire and exclamations like "We kinda fucked them up." The environment transitioned again to the lush A Site, where Phoenix again employed 'Run It Back' to eliminate all opponents, achieving another 'ACE' to the player's excited "Who's next?" and "Let's go!" The segment concluded with a black screen displaying promotional text for Spotify and Twitter, accompanied by the commencement of an upbeat background music track.

Mini-map.description: A circular overhead map in the top-left corner showing player positions, site locations (C Long, C Site, A Site), and enemy movements. Consistently displayed throughout the gameplay, aiding in tactical navigation and site control tracking.

Gelato Shop.description: A colorful storefront with a sign reading 'GELATO' in the outdoor area, serving as a visual landmark during combat. Features a striped awning and ice cream display, adding to the Mediterranean aesthetic of the map.

Vandal Rifle.description: A primary weapon with a dynamic skin that changes from a blue and pink design to a gold and black design. Its precise shots are consistently accompanied by rapid gunfire sounds during combat sequences.

Phoenix.description: The player character, identifiable by fiery orange hands during ability use and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem on the back. Equipped with a Vandal rifle featuring a dynamic skin that changes from blue and pink to gold and black. Uses 'Run It Back' ability multiple times, shown by glowing orange fists and time-reversal effects, and secures an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies. Throughout the segment, Phoenix's player provides tactical callouts like "He's behind us," "One enemy remaining," and "Spike down A," expresses reactions such as "Nice," "Oof," "We kinda fucked them up," and "Let's go," and speaks in Japanese with phrases like "気をつけな" (Be careful), "残り1名" (One enemy remaining), and "パーフェクト" (Perfect).

Run It Back Ability.description: Phoenix’s signature ability, visually represented by fiery orange hands and a time-reversal effect. Activated multiple times to recover from near-death situations, with a cooldown bar visible on the HUD, allowing him to secure an 'ACE' by eliminating all enemies.

Promotional Text Overlays.description: On-screen text in bold white font with black outlines, promoting Spotify and Twitter with phrases like 'Spotify: Rawzu' and 'twitter.com/rawzu', displayed on a black screen at the end of the segment, indicating the player's social media handles.

A Site Environment.description: A lush, green outdoor area with overgrown vegetation, stone pathways, and ancient-style architecture, contrasting with the earlier Mediterranean-style B Site. Features vibrant lighting and natural elements like moss-covered walls and water features, enhancing the visual atmosphere during combat.

Japanese HUD Text.description: On-screen text in Japanese, such as 'スパイク設置完了' (Spike planted) and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能' (Primary weapon unusable), which visually reinforces the Japanese language spoken by the player, including "気をつけな" (Be careful), "残り1名" (One enemy remaining), and "パーフェクト" (Perfect).

raw_segments: The segment opens with Phoenix, sporting fiery orange hands and a white jacket with an orange flame emblem, navigating the B Site of a Mediterranean-style map, wielding a blue and pink Vandal rifle near a Gelato shop. Player callouts like "He's behind us" and "One enemy remaining" guide the action as gunshots ring out, leading to a kill and a 'SPIKE PLANTED' notification. Phoenix then activates his 'Run It Back' ability, marked by glowing orange fists and a time-reversal effect, recovering from a near-death situation amidst more gunfire and player reactions of "Nice" and "Oof," ultimately securing an 'ACE'. The scene transitions to a Japanese server, indicated by HUD text like 'スパイク設置完了' and 'プライマリウェポン使用不能', where Phoenix, now with a gold and black Vandal, moves through a modern industrial corridor. The player's voice shifts to Japanese, uttering "気をつけな" (Be careful), "残り1名" (One enemy remaining), and "パーフェクト" (Perfect) during intense close-quarters combat, punctuated by continuous gunfire and exclamations like "We kinda fucked them up." The environment changes again to the lush A Site, where Phoenix once more utilizes 'Run It Back' to eliminate all enemies, achieving another 'ACE' to the player's excited "Who's next?" and "Let's go!" The segment concludes with a black screen displaying promotional text for Spotify and Twitter, accompanied by the start of an upbeat background music track.

event_blocks.summary: The video concludes with a static black screen displaying promotional text overlays: 'Spotify: Rawzu' in bright green and 'twitter.com/rawzuu' in light blue, both featuring a subtle glow effect. This end-card directs viewers to the player's social media presence without any accompanying audio.

Promotional Text Overlays.description: On-screen text in bold white font with black outlines, promoting Spotify and Twitter with phrases like 'Spotify: Rawzu' and 'twitter.com/rawzu', displayed on a black screen at the end of the segment, indicating the player's social media handles.

raw_segments: The video segment transitions to a black screen, presenting centered, static text overlays. 'Spotify: Rawzu' is displayed in bright green, and 'twitter.com/rawzuu' in light blue, both rendered in a clean, sans-serif font with a subtle glow effect. This visual serves as an end-card or outro sequence, signaling the conclusion of gameplay footage and directly calling viewers to follow the player on social platforms. Throughout this static display, there is no discernible audio, including background music, environmental sounds, or speech.
